task_completion.visual_evidence: The Wazuh dashboard interface is visible in a web browser, indicating successful login.

task_completion.description: Create a CDB list containing the Feodo Tracker C2 IPs
task_completion.visual_evidence: Wazuh UI showing the 'CDB lists' management section with a newly created list (e.g., feodotracker.txt) that contains IP addresses formatted for CDB (e.g., '192.168.1.1:').

task_completion.description: Update the Wazuh configuration to include the new CDB list
task_completion.visual_evidence: Wazuh configuration editor (ossec.conf) showing a <list> tag entry pointing to the newly created CDB list file within the <ruleset> section.

task_completion.description: Create a custom detection rule that references the CDB list
task_completion.visual_evidence: Wazuh 'Custom rules' editor showing an XML rule definition that uses the <list> tag (e.g., <list field="srcip" lookup="address_match_key">) to match network activity against the CDB list.

task_completion.description: Ensure the custom detection rule has a severity level of 9 or above
task_completion.visual_evidence: The custom rule definition explicitly contains a level attribute of 9 or higher (e.g., <rule id="100001" level="9">).

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual Wazuh web interface to configure the rules and lists
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Wazuh dashboard UI (Management > Configuration/Rules/CDB lists), not just local text editors.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used actual IP data from the provided file
integrity.visual_evidence: The CDB list contents shown in the UI should contain real IP addresses extracted from the blocklist, not generic placeholders like '1.1.1.1' or 'IP_ADDRESS'.

integrity.description: Verify the rule and configuration were actually saved in Wazuh
integrity.visual_evidence: The UI should show the rule/CDB list in the saved lists/rules directory, or show a 'Restart manager' prompt/confirmation indicating the changes were applied.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires integrating at least 1 valid IP entry from the Feodo Tracker C2 blocklist into a Wazuh CDB list. The custom detection rule created must have a severity level of 9 or higher to receive full credit.
